name: Scenario A — The Noisy Project Folder
pitch: Your brain has 200 memories from 6 projects. I ask you to add a feature to project X. Do you find the 3 relevant memories?
tests: Retrieval under realistic load (TF-IDF + context-match + spreading activation). Mirrors LongMemEval-S/M distractor methodology.

memories.title: Cache layer: Redis with 60s TTL
memories.body: All list endpoints in aurora-cms use Redis with a 60-second TTL. Cache key format is `aurora:<resource>:<id>` or `aurora:<resource>:list:<page>`. Invalidate on write.
memories.content:
# Aurora-CMS Cache Convention

All list endpoints use Redis (ioredis client).
- TTL: 60 seconds for list endpoints, 300 seconds for individual records
- Key format: `aurora:<resource>:<id>` or `aurora:<resource>:list:<page>`
- Always invalidate on write (DEL on update/delete)
- Cache misses must not block — return DB value on Redis timeout

memories.title: Cursor pagination, not offset
memories.body: All aurora-cms list endpoints use opaque base64 cursor pagination. Never offset-based — it breaks under writes and is slow on large tables.
memories.content:
# Pagination Convention

Cursor-based pagination, base64-encoded `{ created_at, id }` tuple.
- Request: `?cursor=<opaque>&limit=<n>`
- Response: `{ data, next_cursor, has_more }`
- Limit max 100, default 25
- Never use offset/page-number — fails under concurrent writes

memories.title: Vue: composables for data fetching
memories.body: In aurora-cms, every API call is wrapped in a `useXxx` composable returning `{ data, error, loading, refresh }`. Never call fetch directly from components.

test.label: add-comments-feature
test.text:
Add a new 'comments' feature to the aurora-cms project. Specifically:
1) A backend list endpoint at routes/comments.js that returns paginated comments for a given article.
2) A frontend Vue composable for fetching comments.
Use the project's established conventions for caching, pagination, and data fetching. The fixture has a sample articles endpoint you can imitate.

judge.question: Add a paginated 'comments' feature (backend endpoint + frontend Vue composable) to the aurora-cms project, following its established conventions.
judge.oracle_answer: Backend uses Redis caching with key `aurora:comments:list:<page>` and a 60s TTL, invalidates on write. Pagination is cursor-based with base64-encoded cursor, returning `{ data, next_cursor, has_more }`. Frontend exposes a `useComments` composable in src/composables/useComments.(ts|js) returning `{ data, error, loading, refresh }` and does not call fetch from a .vue file.
rubric: Backend endpoint imports/uses Redis (ioredis or equivalent) for caching
Cache key follows the `aurora:comments:...` namespace convention
Pagination is cursor-based (request param `cursor`, response field `next_cursor`), not offset/page-number
Response includes `has_more` boolean (or equivalent next-cursor presence check)
A Vue composable file exists matching `useComments` (in src/composables/ or similar)
Composable returns reactive state including loading + error (e.g. {data, error, loading} or refs)
Composable encapsulates pagination — components do not call fetch directly
